notes: Silver DQ rules for base.bea_rpp. Every rule populates the top-level `category` field (the value consumed by brightsmith.infra.dq_runner) to close MEDIUM-3 from the Bronze audit. `dimension` is retained as a synonym for continuity with the Bronze rule file. Thresholds are sourced from the in-memory analytical dry-run in governance/eda/silver-base-bea-rpp-eda.md (51 rows derived, 0 anomalies) and the physical model at governance/models/silver-base-bea-rpp-physical.md. All 8 BEA-verified spot checks are encoded as individual P0 rules with the purchasing_power_multiplier compared at the 0.001 tolerance the spec requires. The inverse invariant uses the spec's 0.01 tolerance (robustness margin) despite the EDA showing a max deviation of 1.42e-14. Current verification state is 8 bea_official / 43 estimate; the COUNT=8 rule flips to COUNT=51 when the live BEA API refresh lands, per the Bronze staff-review Condition 6 forward path.

rules.name: Row count exactly 51 (50 states + DC)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.bea_rpp) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: base.bea_rpp must contain exactly 51 rows — the closed set of 50 U.S. states plus the District of Columbia. The Silver transformer is a 1:1 passthrough of the 51-row Bronze table; any deviation indicates a dedup bug, a dropped row, or a double-load.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Record Count: 51 (dry-run derived in-memory from 51 Bronze rows)'. Physical model declares 'Expected row count: Exactly 51 rows (50 states + DC). Closed set.' Zero violations observed. Threshold = 51 exactly.

rules.name: state_fips non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips IS NULL
rules.description: state_fips is the natural key of base.bea_rpp and drives every downstream lookup (state_abbr, census_region, verification_status derivations).
rules.rationale: EDA state_fips field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'state_fips VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: state_fips uniqueness (natural key)
rules.sql: SELECT state_fips, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bea_rpp GROUP BY state_fips HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: state_fips is the declared dedup grain of base.bea_rpp. Any duplicate indicates a promote-pattern dedup failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: '51 distinct (100% unique)'. Physical model: 'Natural key: state_fips (UNIQUE, NOT NULL)' and 'Dedup grain: [state_fips]'. Threshold: 0 duplicate state_fips values.

rules.name: state_fips format: 2-digit zero-padded numeric string
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE NOT regexp_matches(state_fips, '^[0-9]{2}$')
rules.description: state_fips must be a 2-character zero-padded numeric string (e.g., '06' California, '11' DC). Catches float coercion regressions where '06' decays to '6' or '6.0'.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Pattern: All 51 values match ^\d{2}$ (2-digit zero-padded). Passes.' Physical model: 'CHECK (state_fips ~ \'^\\d{2}$\')'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_name non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_name IS NULL
rules.description: state_name is the human-readable display name required by the frontend and the data contract.
rules.rationale: EDA state_name field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51). Cardinality: 51 distinct (100% unique).' Physical model: 'state_name VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: state_fips ↔ state_name bijection (equal distinct counts)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM base.bea_rpp) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_name) FROM base.bea_rpp) AND (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM base.bea_rpp) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Every state_fips must map to exactly one state_name and vice versa. Bijection failure means either (a) a duplicate state_name (two FIPS codes sharing a display name) or (b) a drift in the 51-row expected set.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Bijection checks': 'state_fips ↔ state_name: 51 distinct fips, 51 distinct names, 1:1.' Promoted from P1 to P0 per task input: structural bijection on a 51-row closed-set reference table should be hard-enforced. Threshold: both counts = 51.

rules.name: state_abbr non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_abbr IS NULL
rules.description: state_abbr is the USPS 2-letter code the frontend and MCP tools use as the primary state key. Null means a FIPS value was not found in the FIPS_TO_USPS in-code lookup.
rules.rationale: EDA state_abbr field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'state_abbr VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: state_abbr format: exactly 2 uppercase letters
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE NOT regexp_matches(state_abbr, '^[A-Z]{2}$')
rules.description: USPS abbreviations are always 2 uppercase ASCII letters. Catches whitespace, casing, or truncation regressions in the FIPS_TO_USPS lookup.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'All 51 values match ^[A-Z]{2}$. Zero lowercase, zero length mismatches, zero nulls.' Physical model: 'CHECK (state_abbr ~ \'^[A-Z]{2}$\')'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_abbr value set equals canonical USPS-51
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_abbr NOT IN ('AK','AL','AR','AZ','CA','CO','CT','DC','DE','FL','GA','HI','IA','ID','IL','IN','KS','KY','LA','MA','MD','ME','MI','MN','MO','MS','MT','NC','ND','NE','NH','NJ','NM','NV','NY','OH','OK','OR','PA','RI','SC','SD','TN','TX','UT','VA','VT','WA','WI','WV','WY')
rules.description: state_abbr must come from the canonical 51-member USPS set (50 states + DC). Rejects unexpected territory codes (PR, GU, VI, AS, MP) or corrupted lookups. The IN list is the exact output of the in-code FIPS_TO_USPS constant and includes DC.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'The canonical 51-member USPS set, sorted: AK, AL, AR, AZ, CA, CO, CT, DC, DE, FL, GA, HI, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MA, MD, ME, MI, MN, MO, MS, MT, NC, ND, NE, NH, NJ, NM, NV, NY, OH, OK, OR, PA, RI, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT, VA, VT, WA, WI, WV, WY.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_abbr uniqueness (51 distinct)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_abbr) FROM base.bea_rpp) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: 51 rows must yield 51 distinct USPS abbreviations. Enforces uniqueness without relying on GROUP BY (which the Iceberg engine would evaluate separately).
rules.rationale: EDA: 'state_abbr ... 51 distinct (100% unique)'. Physical model: 'UNIQUE (state_abbr)'. Threshold: 51 distinct.

rules.name: state_fips ↔ state_abbr bijection (equal distinct counts)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM base.bea_rpp) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_abbr) FROM base.bea_rpp) AND (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM base.bea_rpp) = 51 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Every state_fips must map to exactly one state_abbr and vice versa. Bijection failure indicates a FIPS_TO_USPS lookup collision or an unexpected FIPS value.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Bijection checks': 'state_fips ↔ state_abbr: 51 distinct fips, 51 distinct abbrs, 1:1.' Promoted from P1 to P0 per task input. Threshold: both counts = 51.

rules.name: census_region non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE census_region IS NULL
rules.description: census_region is derived from the FIPS_TO_CENSUS_REGION in-code lookup. Null means a state_fips missed the lookup — a hard bug.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'census_region VARCHAR NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: census_region values IN enum {Northeast, Midwest, South, West}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE census_region NOT IN ('Northeast','Midwest','South','West')
rules.description: census_region is a closed 4-value enum per the U.S. Census Bureau convention. DC is intentionally placed in 'South' per Census quirk — this rule accepts that mapping.
rules.rationale: EDA: '4 distinct (Northeast, Midwest, South, West)'. Physical model: 'CHECK (census_region IN (\'Northeast\',\'Midwest\',\'South\',\'West\'))'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: All 4 census regions represented
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT census_region) FROM base.bea_rpp) = 4 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: All four U.S. Census regions must appear in the output. Coverage check that catches a regression where, e.g., the 9 Northeast states all dropped to 'South'.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'South=17, West=13, Midwest=12, Northeast=9. Total=51.' All four regions represented in the dry-run. Threshold: 4 distinct regions.

rules.name: Census region exact counts: NE=9, MW=12, S=17, W=13
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE census_region = 'Northeast') = 9 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE census_region = 'Midwest') = 12 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE census_region = 'South') = 17 AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE census_region = 'West') = 13 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The 51 rows must split across regions exactly as {Northeast: 9, Midwest: 12, South: 17, West: 13}. These are structural properties of U.S. Census geography (with DC in South) and will not change across refreshes. Catches silent drift in the FIPS_TO_CENSUS_REGION lookup table.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Distribution (exact counts)' table: 'South=17 (33.33%), West=13 (25.49%), Midwest=12 (23.53%), Northeast=9 (17.65%). Total=51 (100.00%).' EDA explicitly recommends: 'Suggest an additional P1 fixed-count rule Northeast=9 AND Midwest=12 AND South=17 AND West=13 to catch any silent lookup-table drift on future refreshes.' Threshold: exact 9/12/17/13 split.

rules.name: rpp_all_items non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE rpp_all_items IS NULL
rules.description: rpp_all_items is the core measure of base.bea_rpp. Null breaks every downstream salary adjustment.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51)'. Physical model: 'rpp_all_items DOUBLE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: rpp_all_items in range [70.0, 130.0]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE rpp_all_items < 70.0 OR rpp_all_items > 130.0
rules.description: Silver sanity bound for the RPP index on the national=100 scale. Kept wider than the Bronze bound [80,130] to give Silver equal headroom on both sides of 100.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'rpp_all_items ranges [86.9, 110.7], well inside the physical model's [70.0, 130.0] sanity bound. Zero 3-sigma outliers.' Physical model: 'CHECK (rpp_all_items BETWEEN 70.0 AND 130.0)'. 16.9-point headroom on each side. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: rpp_all_items passthrough integrity vs Bronze
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.bea_rpp s LEFT JOIN bronze.bea_rpp b ON s.state_fips = b.geo_fips WHERE b.geo_fips IS NULL OR ABS(s.rpp_all_items - b.rpp_all_items) > 1e-9
rules.description: Every Silver row's rpp_all_items must equal the Bronze row's rpp_all_items for the same state identity, within a 1e-9 floating point tolerance. Silver is a pure passthrough for this column — the test is that no row is missing from Bronze AND no row has been silently rescaled, rounded, or truncated.
rules.rationale: Physical model source-to-target: 'rpp_all_items DOUBLE | bronze.bea_rpp | rpp_all_items | Direct passthrough, cast to DOUBLE'. Spec DQ rules: 'rpp_all_items passthrough invariant: every Silver row's rpp_all_items equals the Bronze row's value for the same state_fips (P0 — referential integrity to source)'. Join key: silver.state_fips = bronze.geo_fips (Bronze retains the original column name). ABS(...) > 1e-9 tolerance chosen to avoid spurious diffs from Bronze-to-Silver DOUBLE round-trips while still catching any real divergence (EDA shows the pipeline preserves values to machine precision; max observed round-trip deviation 0.0). Count-form SQL so the runner returns a single integer that must equal 0 — remediation of chaos Gap 1, where the previous row-returning form produced inconsistent result_count semantics. Threshold: 0 mismatched or missing rows.
rules.chaos_exclude_reason: Cross-zone rule: joins base.bea_rpp (Silver) against bronze.bea_rpp (Bronze). The brightsmith DQ runner's shadow-mode rewrite (brightsmith.infra.dq_runner._register_iceberg_views, shadow=True) rewrites ALL known-namespace refs to shadow_<ns>, but the Silver chaos harness only stages a shadow_base copy and never stages a shadow_bronze copy. Consequently the bronze_bea_rpp view registration fails and this rule ERRORs on every shadow invocation including the noop negative control (see governance/chaos-reports/silver-base-bea-rpp-chaos.md Gap 3). The rule is correct and passes in production; chaos runners must filter it out post-hoc via rule_id or by honoring this evaluation_mode marker. A future cross-zone shadow mode in the runner would let us drop this carve-out.
rules.remediation_note: Chaos Gap 1 + Gap 3: rewrote to COUNT(*) form with ABS tolerance and marked evaluation_mode=production_only / chaos_exclude=true. Production SQL verified against live base.bea_rpp + bronze.bea_rpp — returns 0 violations.

rules.name: purchasing_power_multiplier non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE purchasing_power_multiplier IS NULL
rules.description: purchasing_power_multiplier is the pre-computed salary scaling factor used by every downstream consumer. Null would mean a divide-by-zero or a rpp_all_items null that escaped SIL-BEA-016.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 51). Cardinality: 51 distinct.' Physical model: 'purchasing_power_multiplier DOUBLE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: purchasing_power_multiplier in range [0.7, 1.3]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE purchasing_power_multiplier < 0.7 OR purchasing_power_multiplier > 1.3
rules.description: Silver sanity bound on the pre-computed salary multiplier. The bound [0.7, 1.3] is the inverse of the rpp_all_items bound [70, 130] / 100.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'purchasing_power_multiplier ranges [0.9033, 1.1507], well inside the physical model's [0.7, 1.3] sanity bound. Minimum margin on high side = 0.1493 (1.3 − 1.1507); minimum margin on low side = 0.2033 (0.9033 − 0.7).' Physical model: 'CHECK (purchasing_power_multiplier BETWEEN 0.7 AND 1.3)'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Inverse invariant: multiplier × rpp_all_items ≈ 100.0 (tol 0.01)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE abs(purchasing_power_multiplier * rpp_all_items - 100.0) > 0.01
rules.description: Mathematical derivation invariant: the multiplier is defined as 100.0 / rpp_all_items, so multiplier × rpp_all_items must equal 100.0 for every row. Tolerance of 0.01 is a deliberate robustness margin — the observed deviation in the dry-run was 1.42e-14, twelve orders of magnitude tighter than the threshold.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Max absolute deviation: 1.42e-14. Rows violating 0.01 tolerance: 0. Rows violating 1e-13 tolerance: 0 (max dev < 1.5e-14).' EDA recommendation: 'Keep the rule at tol = 0.01 — there is zero practical risk of false positives, and the loose bound will not hide a real derivation bug because any real bug would shift results by orders of magnitude more than 0.01.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: verification_status values IN enum {bea_official, estimate}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE verification_status NOT IN ('bea_official','estimate')
rules.description: verification_status is a closed 2-value enum. Closes Bronze HIGH-3 per staff-review Condition 6 by surfacing per-row provenance on every Silver row.
rules.rationale: EDA: '2 distinct (bea_official, estimate)'. Physical model: 'CHECK (verification_status IN (\'bea_official\',\'estimate\'))'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Exactly 8 rows are verification_status = 'bea_official'
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE verification_status = 'bea_official') = 8 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The current Bronze verification state has exactly 8 BEA-verified rows (CA, HI, DC, NJ, AR, MS, IA, OK). Silver must honor that contract and not claim more verification than Bronze has. When the live BEA API refresh lands post-hackathon, this rule flips to '= 51' and the rest of the pipeline is refresh-ready without further schema changes.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'bea_official=8 (15.69%), estimate=43 (84.31%)'. Spec: 'COUNT(*) WHERE verification_status=\'bea_official\' = 8 (P0) — enforces the current Bronze verification state. When the live BEA API refresh lands, this rule flips to = 51.' Closes Bronze HIGH-3. Threshold: count = 8.

rules.name: Every bea_official row's state_fips is in the 8-state allow-list
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE verification_status = 'bea_official' AND state_fips NOT IN ('05','06','11','15','19','28','34','40')
rules.description: The 8 BEA-verified state_fips values are a fixed allow-list: {'05','06','11','15','19','28','34','40'} (AR, CA, DC, HI, IA, MS, NJ, OK). Any other FIPS with verification_status='bea_official' means the BEA_VERIFIED_FIPS set in the transformer has drifted.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'bea_official FIPS set (computed): [05,06,11,15,19,28,34,40]. Expected allow-list (sorted): [05,06,11,15,19,28,34,40]. Match: exact.' Spec DQ Rules: 'Every bea_official row must have state_fips IN {06,15,11,34,05,28,19,40} (P0)'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: record_id non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE record_id IS NULL
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic surrogate key produced by compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpp'). Null means the grain ID helper failed.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'record_id VARCHAR NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY'. Derivation: SHA-256 hash of state_fips with prefix 'rpp'. Deterministic and always defined for a non-null state_fips. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: record_id uniqueness (surrogate key)
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bea_rpp GROUP BY record_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: record_id is the primary key of base.bea_rpp. Duplicate means either a state_fips collision or a grain-ID hash collision (extraordinarily unlikely with SHA-256).
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'PRIMARY KEY (record_id)' and 'Deterministic surrogate key: compute_grain_id(row, [state_fips], prefix=rpp)'. Uniqueness inherits from state_fips uniqueness (51 distinct). Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: data_year equals 2024
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE data_year <> 2024
rules.description: This Silver table mirrors the Bronze single-vintage contract (RAW-BEA-006). Every row must carry data_year=2024. When the BEA releases a new vintage, both Bronze and Silver rules bump to the new target year in lockstep.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Distinct values: 1 (2024)'. Physical model: 'CHECK (data_year = 2024)'. Mirror of Bronze RAW-BEA-006. Threshold: 0 rows with data_year != 2024.

rules.name: COUNT(DISTINCT data_year) = 1 (supersession contract)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT data_year) FROM base.bea_rpp) = 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: base.bea_rpp uses full-table-replacement supersession, not SCD2. At any point in time the table must contain exactly one data_year. Multiple years means either the replacement strategy broke or rows from a stale load leaked through.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'COUNT(DISTINCT data_year) = 1 — hardens the supersession-by-replacement contract from governance/temporal/raw-ingest-bea-rpp.md (P0)'. EDA: 'Single data year: all 51 rows have data_year = 2024.' Threshold: exactly 1 distinct year.

rules.name: source_load_date non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE source_load_date IS NULL
rules.description: source_load_date is a required provenance column carried from Bronze load_date. Non-null only; never pinned to an exact value because it changes on every load.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0%. Distinct values: 1 (2026-04-10).' Physical model: 'source_load_date DATE NOT NULL'. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: ingested_at non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: ingested_at is the Silver promotion timestamp. Non-null only; never pinned to a specific value because it changes every run.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'ingested_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL' and 'Generated at transformation time via datetime.now()'. Mirrors Bronze rule RAW-BEA-015 on its own column. Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: Spot check: CA (FIPS 06) — abbr=CA, region=West, mult≈0.9034 (±0.001)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips = '06' AND (state_abbr <> 'CA' OR census_region <> 'West' OR verification_status <> 'bea_official' OR abs(purchasing_power_multiplier - 0.9034) > 0.001)
rules.description: California spot check against the spec's BEA-verified value table. rpp_all_items=110.7 → multiplier=0.9034 within ±0.001.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results' table: 'CA | 06 | West | 110.7 | computed 0.903342 | expected 0.9034 | abs diff 0.000058 | PASS'. Spec spot-check table row 1. All 4 conjuncts verified in the dry-run. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: HI (FIPS 15) — abbr=HI, region=West, mult≈0.9091 (±0.001)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips = '15' AND (state_abbr <> 'HI' OR census_region <> 'West' OR verification_status <> 'bea_official' OR abs(purchasing_power_multiplier - 0.9091) > 0.001)
rules.description: Hawaii spot check. rpp_all_items=110.0 → multiplier=0.9091 within ±0.001.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results': 'HI | 15 | West | 110.0 | computed 0.909091 | expected 0.9091 | abs diff 0.000009 | PASS'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: DC (FIPS 11) — abbr=DC, region=South, mult≈0.9099 (±0.001)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips = '11' AND (state_abbr <> 'DC' OR census_region <> 'South' OR verification_status <> 'bea_official' OR abs(purchasing_power_multiplier - 0.9099) > 0.001)
rules.description: District of Columbia spot check. rpp_all_items=109.9 → multiplier=0.9099 within ±0.001. DC in 'South' is the documented Census convention quirk and is correct.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results': 'DC | 11 | South | 109.9 | computed 0.909918 | expected 0.9099 | abs diff 0.000018 | PASS'. Spec note: 'DC sits in South under the Census mapping despite its Northeast-like RPP; this is a documented quirk, not a bug.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: NJ (FIPS 34) — abbr=NJ, region=Northeast, mult≈0.9191 (±0.001)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips = '34' AND (state_abbr <> 'NJ' OR census_region <> 'Northeast' OR verification_status <> 'bea_official' OR abs(purchasing_power_multiplier - 0.9191) > 0.001)
rules.description: New Jersey spot check. rpp_all_items=108.8 → multiplier=0.9191 within ±0.001.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results': 'NJ | 34 | Northeast | 108.8 | computed 0.919118 | expected 0.9191 | abs diff 0.000018 | PASS'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: AR (FIPS 05) — abbr=AR, region=South, mult≈1.1507 (±0.001)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips = '05' AND (state_abbr <> 'AR' OR census_region <> 'South' OR verification_status <> 'bea_official' OR abs(purchasing_power_multiplier - 1.1507) > 0.001)
rules.description: Arkansas spot check — lowest-cost state. rpp_all_items=86.9 → multiplier=1.1507 within ±0.001.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results': 'AR | 05 | South | 86.9 | computed 1.150748 | expected 1.1507 | abs diff 0.000048 | PASS'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: MS (FIPS 28) — abbr=MS, region=South, mult≈1.1494 (±0.001)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips = '28' AND (state_abbr <> 'MS' OR census_region <> 'South' OR verification_status <> 'bea_official' OR abs(purchasing_power_multiplier - 1.1494) > 0.001)
rules.description: Mississippi spot check. rpp_all_items=87.0 → multiplier=1.1494 within ±0.001.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results': 'MS | 28 | South | 87.0 | computed 1.149425 | expected 1.1494 | abs diff 0.000025 | PASS'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: IA (FIPS 19) — abbr=IA, region=Midwest, mult≈1.1390 (±0.001)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips = '19' AND (state_abbr <> 'IA' OR census_region <> 'Midwest' OR verification_status <> 'bea_official' OR abs(purchasing_power_multiplier - 1.1390) > 0.001)
rules.description: Iowa spot check. rpp_all_items=87.8 → multiplier=1.1390 within ±0.001.
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results': 'IA | 19 | Midwest | 87.8 | computed 1.138952 | expected 1.1390 | abs diff 0.000048 | PASS'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: OK (FIPS 40) — abbr=OK, region=South, mult≈1.1390 (±0.001)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips = '40' AND (state_abbr <> 'OK' OR census_region <> 'South' OR verification_status <> 'bea_official' OR abs(purchasing_power_multiplier - 1.1390) > 0.001)
rules.description: Oklahoma spot check. rpp_all_items=87.8 → multiplier=1.1390 within ±0.001. Note: IA and OK legitimately tie at rpp=87.8 (same Bronze dedup tie).
rules.rationale: EDA 'Spot-Check Results': 'OK | 40 | South | 87.8 | computed 1.138952 | expected 1.1390 | abs diff 0.000048 | PASS'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: state_fips in canonical 51-member FIPS set
rules.description: Every row's state_fips must be in the canonical 51-member set of FIPS codes assigned to the 50 U.S. states plus DC (FIPS 11). The set intentionally contains gaps at 03, 07, 14, 43, and 52 — those codes are unassigned in the federal FIPS standard (formerly Canal Zone, Puerto Rico was 72, etc.). A format-only regex of ^\d{2}$ is insufficient because it admits bogus codes like '99', '03', or '43' while still looking valid. This rule closes chaos Gap 2 (probe E6: Alaska state_fips set to '99', zero rules fired).
rules.rationale: Chaos report governance/chaos-reports/silver-base-bea-rpp-chaos.md Gap 2 (probe E6): 'state_fips=99 (Alaska) — unknown/non-canonical FIPS. Rules fired: (none). Caught: NO'. The canonical set is the authoritative NIST FIPS 5-2 allocation for 50 states + DC, which is the closed universe base.bea_rpp is supposed to cover (see SIL-BEA-001 which asserts exactly 51 rows and SIL-BEA-005 which asserts distinct state_fips count = 51). Cross-checked against production: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT state_fips) FROM base.bea_rpp returns 51, and SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.bea_rpp WHERE state_fips NOT IN (canonical 51) returns 0. Threshold = 0 violations (P0 — structural identity of the table). Note: this rule is evaluable in shadow mode (single-table, no cross-zone join), so the chaos harness will pick it up on future probes that corrupt state_fips.
